title: Authentication, Resource Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Reader Privacy · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers authentication, resource authorization, tenant isolation, and reader privacy into a changed privacy-minimized tenant authorization system. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of privacy-minimized tenant authorization system with automated and judgment evi…
objectives: Verify issuer, audience, allowed algorithm, signature, time, key lifecycle, and transport before creating a minimal readonly principal.
Authorize application commands from principal, tenant, role, ownership, relationship, purpose, and current state rather than route alone.
Require tenant scope in repository methods, SQL, keys, constraints, caches, tests, metrics, exports, support, and admin operations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Token Verification
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 6975 during a clean emitted-package and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove token verification, resource policy, tenant repositories, data minimization, and audit redaction; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant work must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify issuer, audience, allowed algorithm, signature, time, key lifecycle, and transport before creating a minimal readonly principal.

What must be established before implementation?
